notes.content: COLLEGE ADMISSIONS REQUIREMENTS FOR TRANSFER STUDENTS THIS MAJOR IS OFFERED BY THE COLLEGE OF LETTERS AND SCIENCE (L&S) IMPORTANT information for students applying for admission to L&S for the fall 2025 application cycle and beyond Completion of Essential Skills requirements (Reading & Composition, Quantitative Reasoning, and Foreign Language) or Cal-GETC/IGETC will no longer be a requirement for admission; however, completion of either is strongly recommended prior to enrollment at UC Berkeley to ensure timely degree progress. More information on new polices is available on the L&S Transfer Student Applicants website. By the end of the spring term preceding fall enrollment at Berkeley, you must complete : 1) The 7-course pattern (as found in the UC Transfer Admission Eligibility Courses Menu in ASSIST): · Two transferable courses in English composition; · One transferable course in mathematical concepts and quantitative reasoning; · Four transferable college courses chosen from at least two of the following subject areas: o arts and humanities o social and behavioral sciences o physical and biological sciences AND 2) Major Requirements : Follow the guidance on preparation for this major below. For more information on L&S General Education requirements: For information on courses that will satisfy L&S General Education (Essential Skills and Seven-Course Breadth) requirements from your community college complete the following steps: Go to "Search below for articulation agreements: Major, department, and campus-specific general education agreements” Click on the down arrow to select: Academic Year, Institution: UC Berkeley , and Agreements with Other Institutions: (Your CA Community College). Click on “View Agreements”. Under “View Agreement by” select “ General Education/Breadth ”. Click on the “General Education/Breadth” radio button, then click on “View Agreement” Learn more from the UC Berkeley Office of Undergraduate Admission and specifically about transfer admission preparation. Review all available majors at UC Berkeley .
notes.contentHtml:
COLLEGE ADMISSIONS REQUIREMENTS FOR TRANSFER STUDENTS 


THIS MAJOR IS OFFERED BY THE COLLEGE OF LETTERS AND SCIENCE (L&S)


IMPORTANT information for students applying for admission to L&S for the fall 2025 application cycle and beyond


Completion of Essential Skills requirements (Reading & Composition, Quantitative Reasoning, and Foreign Language) or

Cal-GETC/IGETC will no longer be a requirement for admission; however, completion of either is strongly recommended

prior to enrollment at UC Berkeley to ensure timely degree progress. More information on new polices is available on

the L&S Transfer Student Applicants website. 


By the end of the spring term preceding fall enrollment at Berkeley, you must complete: 


1) The 7-course pattern (as found in the UC Transfer Admission Eligibility Courses Menu in ASSIST):

·   Two transferable courses in English composition;

·   One transferable course in mathematical concepts and quantitative reasoning;

·   Four transferable college courses chosen from at least two of the following subject areas:

o arts and humanities

o social and behavioral sciences

o physical and biological sciences


AND


2) Major Requirements: 

Follow the guidance on preparation for this major below. 


For more information on L&S General Education requirements:


For information on courses that will satisfy L&S General Education (Essential Skills and

Seven-Course Breadth) requirements from your community college complete the following steps:

  1. Go to "Search below for articulation agreements: Major, department, and campus-specific general education agreements”
  2. Click on the down arrow to select: Academic Year, Institution: UC Berkeley, and Agreements with Other Institutions: (Your CA Community College). 
  3. Click on “View Agreements”.
  4. Under “View Agreement by” select “General Education/Breadth”. 
  5. Click on the “General Education/Breadth” radio button, then click on “View Agreement”


Learn more from the UC Berkeley Office of Undergraduate Admission and specifically about transfer admission preparation. 


Review all available majors at UC Berkeley.

notes.content: Transfer students who select this major on their College of Letters and Science (L&S) application and are offered admission to UCB L&S will be directly admitted into this major. If you are interested in this major, it is very important to select this major on your application, and complete as many lower-division major requirements as possible prior to transfer. See details on preparation for this major below. REQUIRED COURSES: Complete the approved lower division courses or their equivalents as listed in the “Lower Division Major Preparation Courses” section of this document: · Sociology 1 (Introduction to Sociology) and · One course in either statistics or logic. NOTE: The Sociology major has a total of 3 lower division prerequisites. Although some community colleges offer the equivalent of UCB’s Sociology 5 (Evaluation of Evidence), most do not. If an equivalent for UCB’s Sociology 5 is not available at your California Community College, it is recommended that transfer students take UCB’s Sociology 5 course in their first semester at UC Berkeley. See “Strongly Recommended” section below for more information. STRONGLY RECOMMENDED Although some California community colleges offer the equivalent of UCB’s Sociology 5 (Evaluation of Evidence), most do not. It is recommended that transfer students take UCB’s Sociology 5 course in their first semester at UC Berkeley unless it is available at their community college. For a list of California Community Colleges that offer an equivalent course, please visit the Sociology Major Requirements website and review the information available about the UCB Sociology 5, Evaluation of Evidence requirement. https://sociology.berkeley.edu/major-requirements Berkeley Sociology offers only one concentration in the major, a Sociology Research Method Concentration. The Concentration requires students to earn a letter grade of B+ or higher in five specific courses, which includes Statistics and Sociology 5. Please note that in order to be eligible for the Research Methods Concentration, only Statistics may be completed at a California Community College. For questions, please contact a Sociology major advisor. https://sociology.berkeley.edu/research-methods-concentration Major Preparation · Entering transfer students should plan to complete any remaining prerequisites during their first semester at UC Berkeley. · Students must maintain a cumulative and major grade point average of a 2.0 or higher. · Sociology 1 or its equivalent must be completed with a letter grade of at least a D- or higher. · Sociology 5 or its equivalent must be completed with a letter grade of at least a C- or higher. · Statistics or logic may be completed with a letter grade of D- or higher, or for a “Pass” grade. · Students can remain declared in the major while the last of your prerequisites are in progress at UC Berkeley. · If you have completed course work that has not been approved toward the Berkeley Sociology requirements listed below, you may retain syllabi and reading lists for evaluation by the department. Contact a major advisor for more information. NOTE: The Department of Sociology is not involved in the Admissions process. Students should review UC Berkeley Admissions information in order to determine how to become competitively eligible. For more information on admissions requirements to UC Berkeley, visit the Office of Undergraduate Admissions website: http://admissions.berkeley.edu
notes.contentHtml:
Transfer students who select this major on their College of Letters and Science (L&S) application and are offered admission to UCB L&S will be directly admitted into this major.


If you are interested in this major, it is very important to select this major on your application, and complete as many lower-division major requirements as possible prior to transfer. See details on preparation for this major below.


REQUIRED COURSES:

Complete the approved lower division courses or their equivalents as listed in the “Lower Division Major Preparation Courses” section of this document:


·      Sociology 1 (Introduction to Sociology) and

·      One course in either statistics or logic.

 

NOTE: The Sociology major has a total of 3 lower division prerequisites. Although some community colleges offer the equivalent of UCB’s Sociology 5 (Evaluation of Evidence), most do not. If an equivalent for UCB’s Sociology 5 is not available at your California Community College, it is recommended that transfer students take UCB’s Sociology 5 course in their first semester at UC Berkeley. See “Strongly Recommended” section below for more information.


 

STRONGLY RECOMMENDED

Although some California community colleges offer the equivalent of UCB’s Sociology 5 (Evaluation of Evidence), most do not. It is recommended that transfer students take UCB’s Sociology 5 course in their first semester at UC Berkeley unless it is available at their community college.


For a list of California Community Colleges that offer an equivalent course, please visit the Sociology Major Requirements website and review the information available about the UCB Sociology 5, Evaluation of Evidence requirement.

https://sociology.berkeley.edu/major-requirements


Berkeley Sociology offers only one concentration in the major, a Sociology Research Method Concentration. The Concentration requires students to earn a letter grade of B+ or higher in five specific courses, which includes Statistics and Sociology 5. Please note that in order to be eligible for the Research Methods Concentration, only Statistics may be completed at a California Community College. For questions, please contact a Sociology major advisor.

https://sociology.berkeley.edu/research-methods-concentration


Major Preparation

·      Entering transfer students should plan to complete any remaining prerequisites during their first semester at UC Berkeley.

·      Students must maintain a cumulative and major grade point average of a 2.0 or higher. 

·      Sociology 1 or its equivalent must be completed with a letter grade of at least a D- or higher.

·      Sociology 5 or its equivalent must be completed with a letter grade of at least a C- or higher. 

·      Statistics or logic may be completed with a letter grade of D- or higher, or for a “Pass” grade.

·      Students can remain declared in the major while the last of your prerequisites are in progress at UC Berkeley.

·      If you have completed course work that has not been approved toward the Berkeley Sociology requirements listed below, you may retain syllabi and reading lists for evaluation by the department. Contact a major advisor for more information.


NOTE: The Department of Sociology is not involved in the Admissions process. Students should review UC Berkeley Admissions information in order to determine how to become competitively eligible. For more information on admissions requirements to UC Berkeley, visit the Office of Undergraduate Admissions website: http://admissions.berkeley.edu

notes.content: ● A score of 4 or 5 on the Advanced Placement Statistics Exam can satisfy the Statistics/Logic requirement for the Sociology major. Students must be prepared to provide their AP score report in order to use exam credit for the Statistics/Logic major requirement.
notes.contentHtml: ●     A score of 4 or 5 on the Advanced Placement Statistics Exam can satisfy the Statistics/Logic requirement for the Sociology major. Students must be prepared to provide their AP score report in order to use exam credit for the Statistics/Logic major requirement.
